task_completion.description: Open and view the source inventory and restriction sheets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open showing sheets or files named 'Main_Inventory', 'Family_Promises', or 'Sentimental_Keep'.

task_completion.description: Create a final consolidated estate sale inventory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new sheet, document, or clearly defined section containing the combined list of items from the source data.

The final inventory clearly marks, filters, or separates sentimental items (e.g., Wedding Album, Military Medals, Grandfather Clock, Jewelry Box) as 'keepsake', 'non-sellable', or similar, distinct from items that are for sale.

task_completion.description: Resolve conflicting item assignments for family members.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final inventory shows a resolution, assignment note, or specific handling for the conflicting items: Antique Oak Dresser, Quilt Collection, Piano, and Telescope.

task_completion.description: Apply a uniform price format to all items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The price column in the final inventory uses a consistent, uniform format for all entries (e.g., all formatted as $X.XX with no mixed currency symbols or missing decimals).

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI spreadsheet application to organize the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet UI (like LibreOffice Calc) rather than a terminal window running a Python script to bypass manual/GUI work.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the final answers into a basic text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final inventory is presented in a structured spreadsheet or document format, not just a plain text file with pasted answers.

integrity.description: The agent actively manipulated the data to achieve the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible signs of data formatting, column creation, sorting, or formula usage in the spreadsheet application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source data contains 42 items. There are exactly 4 conflicting items promised to multiple people: Antique Oak Dresser, Quilt Collection, Piano, and Telescope. There are exactly 8 sentimental keepsakes that must not be sold: Wedding Album, Military Medals, Photo Albums, Grandmother's China Set, Grandfather Clock, Piano, Quilt Collection, and Jewelry Box. Note that 'Piano' and 'Quilt Collection' are both conflicting and sentimental. The final inventory must correctly identify and handle these specific items.
